customer_anxiety_profile: Andy is not anxious — he is an informed, self-directed prospect who has evaluated solar before and returned with updated assumptions. His primary concern is economic viability (prior numbers didn't work; technology has changed). He is not skeptical of Ion specifically but is doing his own due diligence. No scam anxiety, no urgency pressure.
unresolved_concerns: Net metering policy for Rappahannock Electric Cooperative — Andy asked directly and received a hedged, uncertain answer. This is unresolved and will be the first question he asks when re-engaged.
Ground-mount economics — Corey dismissed ground mounts as 'super expensive' without a specialist's assessment. Andy owns land and may revisit this.
Whether the existing home at 376 Seville Road qualifies for solar — never explored on this call.
Tax credit / federal incentive status — Andy raised this; Corey offered a vague 'our own program' without specifics.
sensitive_topics_raised: Prior negative solar evaluation ('the numbers really didn't make sense a few years ago') — Andy is returning with updated assumptions but will be watching for the same outcome.
Rappahannock Electric Cooperative net metering policy — Andy has specific knowledge of this utility's historical limitations on selling back power.
exact_promises_rep_made: Corey will send a text message with his personal number so Andy can reach him directly once the workshop roof design is finalized.
Ion can help with battery storage systems ('Of course. Yes.').
closer_should_know: Andy re-engaged twice after Corey's close-out — once on net metering and once on battery storage — which means his interest is real and active despite the soft-callback framing. The existing home at 376 Seville Road was never qualified; that is the first conversation to have. Andy's battery storage interest (Tesla Powerwall) and his Rappahannock Electric Cooperative net metering question are the two hooks that will re-open the conversation. Do not lead with the workshop — lead with the existing home and the battery question.
